rationale: The cited paper is not only listed in background citations but is also discussed specifically as an example of mindset spillover to an unrelated task (broad vs. narrow categorization affecting later fruit grouping). The citing paper uses this prior result to frame what existing mindset work has done (goal striving within an assigned task) and to motivate its own contribution on creativity effects and task choice (goal setting). This gives the citation a clear conceptual positioning role beyond a passing mention.
relation_description: Building on Ulkumen, Chakravarti, and Morwitz (2010) as evidence that induced mindsets transfer to unrelated decisions, the citing paper extends that line to problem-solving mindsets and examines downstream effects on creative performance and subsequent task selection.
